How to set PyMC environment locally

June 9, 2022

In the beginning, there was light, well, in my case, great mentors with a great plan. To achieve all the objectives, I had to make sure that I had everything installed correctly and that I understood how making contributions to PyMC works.

While this official tutorial is very explanatory, I will briefly put down the main steps followed by me, which, of course, mostly coincide with the ones from the tutorial.

  1. Fork the main repository.
  2. Clone it locally either using GitHub Desktop or using the terminal and type
    git clone git@github.com:'your GitHub handle'/pymc.git
  3. Check your working directory by typing pwd
  4. Make sure to be the pymc folder. If not use cd pymc
  5. Write this (I will explain in the next post the local, origin and upstream branches)
    git remote add upstream git@github.com:pymc-devs/pymc.git
  6. Create a virtual environment:
    conda env create -f conda-envs/environment-dev-py39.yml
    conda activate pymc-dev-py39
    pip install -e .
  7. Run pre-commit checks:
    pip install pre-commit
    pre-commit run --all
    pre-commit install
  8. You can also run tests to make sure everything works:
    pytest -v -k test_math pymc/tests

And that’s it 🎉
